ground.schema:
Node properties:
- **Movie**
  - `average_vote`: FLOAT Example: "7.7"
  - `overview`: STRING Example: "Led by Woody, Andy's toys live happily in his room"
  - `revenue`: FLOAT Example: "3.73554033E8"
  - `vote_count`: INTEGER Example: "5415"
  - `tagline`: STRING Example: "Roll the dice and unleash the excitement!"
  - `budget`: INTEGER Example: "30000000"
  - `title`: STRING Example: "Toy Story"
  - `poster_path`: STRING Example: "/rhIRbceoE9lR4veEXuwCC2wARtG.jpg"
  - `imdb_id`: STRING Example: "tt0114709"
  - `status`: STRING Example: "Released"
  - `runtime`: FLOAT Example: "81.0"
  - `popularity`: FLOAT Example: "21.946943"
  - `homepage`: STRING Example: "http://toystory.disney.com/toy-story"
  - `release_date`: DATE Example: "1995-10-30"
  - `id`: INTEGER Min: 862, Max: 31357
  - `original_title`: STRING Example: "Toy Story"
  - `original_language`: STRING Example: "en"
- **Language**
  - `id`: STRING Example: "en"
  - `name`: STRING Available options: ['', 'Gaeilge', 'Kiswahili', 'Azərbaycan', 'Somali', 'беларуская мова', 'Fulfulde', 'Hausa']
- **Country**
  - `id`: STRING Example: "US"
  - `name`: STRING Example: "United States of America"
- **Genre**
  - `id`: INTEGER Min: 12, Max: 10770
  - `name`: STRING Example: "Animation"
- **ProductionCompany**
  - `id`: INTEGER Min: 3, Max: 10201
  - `name`: STRING Example: "Pixar Animation Studios"
- **Collection**
  - `name`: STRING Example: "Toy Story Collection"
  - `id`: INTEGER Min: 10, Max: 480160
  - `backdrop_path`: STRING Example: "/9FBwqcd9IRruEDUrTdcaafOMKUq.jpg"
  - `poster_path`: STRING Example: "/7G9915LfUQ2lVfwMEEhDsn3kT4B.jpg"
- **Person**
  - `gender`: INTEGER Example: "2"
  - `profile_path`: STRING Example: "/pQFoyx7rp09CJTAb932F2g8Nlho.jpg"
  - `id`: INTEGER Min: 31, Max: 12900
  - `name`: STRING Example: "Tom Hanks"
- **User**
  - `id`: INTEGER Min: 27490cf6-22eb-495d-9dd6-902c0f89c627, Max: 4
- **Keyword**
  - `id`: INTEGER Min: 931, Max: 9713
  - `name`: STRING Example: "jealousy"
- **Video**
  - `tagline`: STRING Example: "Fresh Fish. Mixed Vegetables."
  - `average_vote`: FLOAT Min: 0.0, Max: 10.0
  - `overview`: STRING Example: "Get ready as Bob the Tomato, Larry the Cucumber an"
  - `revenue`: FLOAT Min: 0.0, Max: 0.0
  - `poster_path`: STRING Example: "/iWahPsY3hsKOizZHgzQcAxv4O3B.jpg"
  - `vote_count`: INTEGER Min: 0, Max: 115
  - `title`: STRING Example: "Jonah: A VeggieTales Movie"
  - `release_date`: DATE Min: 1973-01-12, Max: 2017-03-17
  - `status`: STRING Available options: ['Released']
  - `budget`: INTEGER Min: 0, Max: 2219684
  - `popularity`: FLOAT Min: 0.0, Max: 9.22546
  - `imdb_id`: STRING Example: "tt0298388"
  - `original_title`: STRING Example: "Jonah: A VeggieTales Movie"
  - `id`: INTEGER Min: 13284, Max: 461088
  - `runtime`: FLOAT Min: 0.0, Max: 583.0
  - `original_language`: STRING Example: "en"
  - `homepage`: STRING Example: "http://www.loosechange911.com/"
- **Adult**
  - `average_vote`: FLOAT Min: 0.0, Max: 6.3
  - `overview`: STRING Available options: ['A sailor takes an American businessman and his gir', 'The FBI and Texas police make ready to storm the h', 'Electrical Girl centers around a horny young woman', 'Ágata suffers from a psychological disorder which ', "Shane Ryan's sequel to the disturbing Amateur Porn", 'Australian film about a fictional sub-par Australi', 'Olaf and his mother run a boarding house and a whi', 'A man who returns home to find his wife cheating o', 'An American scientific research facility opens a p']
  - `revenue`: FLOAT Min: 0.0, Max: 0.0
  - `poster_path`: STRING Available options: ['/tzVK7nTnzUiVeFSZhOAIHmrgXUS.jpg', '/1VzwIa8HTykNiCCW8PBaJnyTvO8.jpg', '/yTfqRQlJMTAuTNDG01XjNg5KPPg.jpg', '/oP2WvKIWxqSBVHjfYAizFHOqBLG.jpg', '/zpycBSiBFp5Uib6gWJpgEvfOgdn.jpg', '/e7CAc8OadcUoKNPIX86SEPSX7f.jpg', '/jjncR0vECvXVrBvr0A1vLghL4pN.jpg', '/pJLmZBruOmn2c9mYWe3h1xcXaA0.jpg', '/3uv9oaqOJkkdZiQp6r9lf24tkHW.jpg']
  - `vote_count`: INTEGER Min: 0, Max: 16
  - `title`: STRING Available options: ['Erotic Nights of the Living Dead', 'Standoff', 'Electrical Girl', 'Diet of Sex', 'Amateur Porn Star Killer 2', 'The Band', 'The Sinful Dwarf', 'Adulterers', 'Half -Life']
  - `release_date`: DATE Min: 1973-12-20, Max: 2016-01-05
  - `status`: STRING Available options: ['Released', 'In Production']
  - `budget`: INTEGER Min: 0, Max: 750000
  - `popularity`: FLOAT Min: 0.001803, Max: 6.889832
  - `imdb_id`: STRING Available options: ['tt0079642', 'tt0119931', 'tt0322232', 'tt3094816', 'tt1161951', 'tt1153101', 'tt0070696', 'tt4044464', 'tt2697688']
  - `original_title`: STRING Available options: ['Le notti erotiche dei morti viventi', 'Standoff', '發電悄嬌娃', 'Diet of Sex', 'Amateur Porn Star Killer 2', 'The Band', 'Dværgen', 'Adulterers', 'Half -Life']
  - `id`: INTEGER Min: 5422, Max: 448290
  - `runtime`: FLOAT Min: 0.0, Max: 112.0
  - `original_language`: STRING Available options: ['it', 'en', 'cn', 'es', 'da']
  - `tagline`: STRING Available options: ['Trust Will Get You Killed', 'Comedy, food, drama and sex, a lot of sex', 'Humiliation. Rape. Murder. You know the drill.', "Sex, drugs and Rock 'n Roll", 'The Mother of all Dwarfsploitation films!', 'Love. Betrayal. Vengeance']
  - `homepage`: STRING Available options: ['http://www.imdb.com/title/tt0119931/', 'http://www.dietofsex.com/']
- **Package**
  - `price`: FLOAT Min: 0.0, Max: 99.99
  - `duration`: DURATION 
  - `id`: INTEGER Min: 0, Max: 6
  - `name`: STRING Available options: ['Childrens', 'Bronze', 'Silver', 'Gold', 'Platinum', 'Ultimate', 'Free Trial']
- **Subscription**
  - `expiresAt`: DATE_TIME Min: 2020-08-23T16:20:52.383Z, Max: 2020-08-23T16:20:52.383Z
  - `id`: STRING Available options: ['e74f7163-eace-4169-8043-41627f1fe236']
Relationship properties:
- **CAST_FOR**
  - `credit_id: STRING` Example: "52fe4459c3a36847f8090d7d"
  - `order: INTEGER` Example: "0"
  - `character: STRING` Example: "Charlie Wilson"
  - `cast_id: INTEGER` Example: "12"
- **CREW_FOR**
  - `credit_id: STRING` Example: "55e4f0abc3a368140800047e"
  - `job: STRING` Example: "Producer"
  - `department: STRING` Example: "Production"
- **RATED**
  - `timestamp: DATE_TIME` Example: "2015-03-09T22:49:42Z"
  - `rating: FLOAT` Example: "5.0"
The relationships:
(:Movie)-[:ORIGINAL_LANGUAGE]->(:Language)
(:Movie)-[:SPOKEN_IN_LANGUAGE]->(:Language)
(:Movie)-[:PRODUCED_IN_COUNTRY]->(:Country)
(:Movie)-[:IN_GENRE]->(:Genre)
(:Movie)-[:PRODUCED_BY]->(:ProductionCompany)
(:Movie)-[:HAS_KEYWORD]->(:Keyword)
(:Movie)-[:IN_COLLECTION]->(:Collection)
(:Person)-[:CAST_FOR]->(:Movie)
(:Person)-[:CREW_FOR]->(:Movie)
(:Person)-[:CREW_FOR]->(:Video)
(:User)-[:RATED]->(:Movie)
(:User)-[:RATED]->(:Video)
(:Video)-[:ORIGINAL_LANGUAGE]->(:Language)
(:Video)-[:SPOKEN_IN_LANGUAGE]->(:Language)
(:Video)-[:PRODUCED_IN_COUNTRY]->(:Country)
(:Video)-[:IN_GENRE]->(:Genre)
(:Video)-[:PRODUCED_BY]->(:ProductionCompany)
(:Video)-[:HAS_KEYWORD]->(:Keyword)
(:Video)-[:IN_COLLECTION]->(:Collection)
(:Adult)-[:ORIGINAL_LANGUAGE]->(:Language)
(:Adult)-[:SPOKEN_IN_LANGUAGE]->(:Language)
(:Adult)-[:PRODUCED_IN_COUNTRY]->(:Country)
(:Adult)-[:IN_GENRE]->(:Genre)
(:Adult)-[:PRODUCED_BY]->(:ProductionCompany)
(:Adult)-[:HAS_KEYWORD]->(:Keyword)
(:Package)-[:PROVIDES_ACCESS_TO]->(:Genre)
(:Subscription)-[:FOR_PACKAGE]->(:Package)
ground.cypher:
MATCH
    p1 = (m:Movie)-[r1:ORIGINAL_LANGUAGE|SPOKEN_IN_LANGUAGE|PRODUCED_IN_COUNTRY|IN_GENRE|PRODUCED_BY|HAS_KEYWORD|IN_COLLECTION]-(*)
    p2 = (m)-[r2:CAST_FOR|CREW_FOR]-(p:Person)
    p3 = (m)-[r3:RATED]-(u:User)
    p4 = (m)-[r4:IN_COLLECTION]-(c:Collection)
    p5 = (v:Video)-[r5:ORIGINAL_LANGUAGE|SPOKEN_IN_LANGUAGE|PRODUCED_IN_COUNTRY|IN_GENRE|PRODUCED_BY|HAS_KEYWORD|IN_COLLECTION]-(*)
    p6 = (v)-[r6:CREW_FOR]-(p2:Person)
    p7 = (v)-[r7:RATED]-(u:User)
    p8 = (v)-[r8:IN_COLLECTION]-(c:Collection)
    p9 = (a:Adult)-[r9:ORIGINAL_LANGUAGE|SPOKEN_IN_LANGUAGE|PRODUCED_IN_COUNTRY|IN_GENRE|PRODUCED_BY|HAS_KEYWORD|IN_COLLECTION]-(*)
    p10=(a)-[r10:IN_COLLECTION]-(c:Collection)

WHERE
    // Ensure we have at least one path from Movie to its related entities
    p1 <> [] AND p2 <> [] AND p3 <> [] AND p4 <> []
    AND p5 <> [] AND p6 <> [] AND p7 <> [] AND p8 <> []
    AND p9 <> [] AND p10 <> []

RETURN
    m.title AS movie_title,
    ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT nlimb END p1) AS movie_languages,
    ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT nlimb END p2) AS movie_actors,
    ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT nlimb END p3) AS movie_rated_users,
    ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT nlimb END p4) AS movie_collections,
    ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT nlimb END p5) AS video_languages,
    ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT nlimb END p6) AS video_crew_members,
    ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT nlimb END p7) AS video_rated_users,
    ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT nlimb END p8) AS video_collections,
    ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT nlimb END p9) AS adult_languages,
    ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT nlimb END p10) AS adult_collections;
ground.question:
Here's an improved Cypher query that properly extracts all relationships for Movies, Videos, and Adult content while ensuring we get meaningful aggregations of related entities:

```cypher
MATCH
    // Get Movies with all their relationships
    (m:Movie)-[r1:ORIGINAL_LANGUAGE|SPOKEN_IN_LANGUAGE|PRODUCED_IN_COUNTRY|IN_GENRE|PRODUCED_BY|HAS_KEYWORD|IN_COLLECTION]-(n1:Country|Language|Genre|ProductionCompany|Keyword|Collection)
    (m)-[r2:CAST_FOR|CREW_FOR]-(p:Person)
    (m)-[r3:RATED]-(u:User)
    (m)-[r4:IN_COLLECTION]-(c:Collection)

    // Get Videos with all their relationships
    (v:Video)-[r5:ORIGINAL_LANGUAGE|SPOKEN_IN_LANGUAGE|PRODUCED_IN_COUNTRY|IN_GENRE|PRODUCED_BY|HAS_KEYWORD|IN_COLLECTION]-(n2:Country|Language|Genre|ProductionCompany|Keyword|Collection)
    (v)-[r6:CREW_FOR]-(p2:Person)
    (v)-[r7:RATED]-(u2:User)
    (v)-[r8:IN_COLLECTION]-(c2:Collection)

    // Get Adult content with all their relationships
    (a:Adult)-[r9:ORIGINAL_LANGUAGE|SPOKEN_IN_LANGUAGE|PRODUCED_IN_COUNTRY|IN_GENRE|PRODUCED_BY|HAS_KEYWORD|IN_COLLECTION]-(n3:Country|Language|Genre|ProductionCompany|Keyword|Collection)
    (a)-[r10:IN_COLLECTION]-(c3:Collection)

WHERE
    // Ensure we have at least one path for each type
    m <> null AND v <> null AND a <> null

RETURN
    // Movie information and relationships
    m.title AS movie_title,
    COALESCE(collect(distinct n1.name), []) AS movie_languages,
    COALESCE(collect(distinct p.name), []) AS movie_actors,
    COALESCE(collect(distinct u.username), []) AS movie_rated_users,
    COALESCE(collect(distinct c.name), []) AS movie_collections,

    // Video information and relationships
    v.title AS video_title,
    COALESCE(collect(distinct n2.name), []) AS video_languages,
    COALESCE(collect(distinct p2.name), []) AS video_crew_members,
    COALESCE(collect(distinct u2.username), []) AS video_rated_users,
    COALESCE(collect(distinct c2.name), []) AS video_collections,

    // Adult content information and relationships
    a.title AS adult_title,
    COALESCE(collect(distinct n3.name), []) AS adult_languages,
    COALESCE(collect(distinct c3.name), []) AS adult_collections;

OPTIONAL MATCH
    // Get package information for subscriptions
    (m)-[:PROVIDES_ACCESS_TO]->(p:Package)
    (v)-[:PROVIDES_ACCESS_TO]->(p:Package)
    (a)-[:PROVIDES_ACCESS_TO]->(p:Package)

RETURN
    // Add package information if available
    COALESCE(collect(distinct p.name), []) AS packages;
```

Key improvements:

1. Separated the queries for Movies, Videos, and Adult content for better clarity
2. Used `COALESCE` to handle cases where collections might be empty
3. Added proper type casting to get names from nodes
4. Simplified the relationship matching by directly using the relationship types
5. Added optional matching for package information if available
6. Used distinct aggregation to avoid duplicates
7. Added proper handling for cases where relationships might not exist
8. Included all relevant relationship types for each content type

The query will return three main groups of results:
- Movie information with its languages, actors, ratings, and collections
- Video information with its languages, crew members, ratings, and collections
- Adult content information with its languages and collections

You can filter the results by content type if you only need specific information.

cypher:
MATCH (m:Movie)-[r1:ORIGINAL_LANGUAGE|SPOKEN_IN_LANGUAGE|PRODUCED_IN_COUNTRY|IN_GENRE|PRODUCED_BY|HAS_KEYWORD|IN_COLLECTION]-(n1:Country|Language|Genre|ProductionCompany|Keyword|Collection)
MATCH (m)-[r2:CAST_FOR|CREW_FOR]-(p:Person)
MATCH (m)-[r3:RATED]-(u:User)
MATCH (m)-[r4:IN_COLLECTION]-(c:Collection)
MATCH (v:Video)-[r5:ORIGINAL_LANGUAGE|SPOKEN_IN_LANGUAGE|PRODUCED_IN_COUNTRY|IN_GENRE|PRODUCED_BY|HAS_KEYWORD|IN_COLLECTION]-(n2:Country|Language|Genre|ProductionCompany|Keyword|Collection)
MATCH (v)-[r6:CREW_FOR]-(p2:Person)
MATCH (v)-[r7:RATED]-(u2:User)
MATCH (v)-[r8:IN_COLLECTION]-(c2:Collection)
MATCH (a:Adult)-[r9:ORIGINAL_LANGUAGE|SPOKEN_IN_LANGUAGE|PRODUCED_IN_COUNTRY|IN_GENRE|PRODUCED_BY|HAS_KEYWORD|IN_COLLECTION]-(n3:Country|Language|Genre|ProductionCompany|Keyword|Collection)
MATCH (a)-[r10:IN_COLLECTION]-(c3:Collection)
WHERE m <> null AND v <> null AND a <> null
RETURN m.title AS movie_title, COALESCE(collect(distinct n1.name), []) AS movie_languages, COALESCE(collect(distinct p.name), []) AS movie_actors, COALESCE(collect(distinct u.username), []) AS movie_rated_users, COALESCE(collect(distinct c.name), []) AS movie_collections, v.title AS video_title, COALESCE(collect(distinct n2.name), []) AS video_languages, COALESCE(collect(distinct p2.name), []) AS video_crew_members, COALESCE(collect(distinct u2.username), []) AS video_rated_users, COALESCE(collect(distinct c2.name), []) AS video_collections, a.title AS adult_title, COALESCE(collect(distinct n3.name), []) AS adult_languages, COALESCE(collect(distinct c3.name), []) AS adult_collections, OPTIONAL MATCH (m)-[:PROVIDES_ACCESS_TO]->(p:Package)
RETURN COALESCE(collect(distinct p.name), []) AS packages
